From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1970 studio album by Gene Ammons

| Brother Jug! | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Studio album](/wiki/Album "Album") by [Gene Ammons](/wiki/Gene_Ammons "Gene Ammons") | |
| Released | 1970 |
| Recorded | November 10–11, 1969 |
| Studio | [Van Gelder Studio](/wiki/Van_Gelder_Studio "Van Gelder Studio"), Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey |
| [Genre](/wiki/Music_genre "Music genre") | [Jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") |
| Length | 33:42 |
| [Label](/wiki/Record_label "Record label") | [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") PR 7792 |
| [Producer](/wiki/Record_producer "Record producer") | [Bob Porter](/wiki/Bob_Porter_(record_producer) "Bob Porter (record producer)") |
| [Gene Ammons](/wiki/Gene_Ammons "Gene Ammons") chronology | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | *[The Boss Is Back!](/wiki/The_Boss_Is_Back! "The Boss Is Back!")* (1969) | ***Brother Jug!*** (1970) | *[Night Lights](/wiki/Night_Lights_(Gene_Ammons_album) "Night Lights (Gene Ammons album)")* (1970) | | |

Professional ratings

| Review scores | |
| --- | --- |
| Source | Rating |
| [AllMusic](/wiki/AllMusic "AllMusic") | StarStarStar[[1]](#cite_note-Allmusic-1) |
| *[The Rolling Stone Jazz Record Guide](/wiki/The_Rolling_Stone_Jazz_Record_Guide "The Rolling Stone Jazz Record Guide")* | StarStarStar[[2]](#cite_note-RSJRG-2) |

***Brother Jug!*** is an album by saxophonist [Gene Ammons](/wiki/Gene_Ammons "Gene Ammons") recorded in 1969 and released on the [Prestige](/wiki/Prestige_Records "Prestige Records") label.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It contains material from the same two dates as *[The Boss Is Back!](/wiki/The_Boss_Is_Back! "The Boss Is Back!")* (1969).

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brother_Jug!&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Reception")]

[AllMusic](/wiki/AllMusic "AllMusic") awarded the album 3 stars with its review by Stewart Mason stating, "A swinging soul-jazz set from just before the point where soul-jazz turned once and for all into fusion, 1970's *Brother Jug* is very much an album of its time... while the album doesn't have the classic timelessness of Gene Ammons' best '50s and early-'60s work, *Brother Jug* is one of Ammons' better albums released soon after the tenor saxophonist's release from a seven-year prison sentence".[[1]](#cite_note-Allmusic-1)

"Jungle Strut" was covered by [Santana](/wiki/Santana_(band) "Santana (band)") in the 1971 album *[Santana III](/wiki/Santana_(1971_album) "Santana (1971 album)")*.

## Track listing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brother_Jug!&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Track listing")]

All compositions by Gene Ammons except where noted.

1. "[Son of a Preacher Man](/wiki/Son_of_a_Preacher_Man "Son of a Preacher Man")" (John Hurley, Ronnie Wilkins) – 4:27
2. "[Didn't We](/wiki/Didn%27t_We_(Lee_Greenwood_song) "Didn't We (Lee Greenwood song)")" ([Graham Lyle](/wiki/Graham_Lyle "Graham Lyle"), [Troy Seals](/wiki/Troy_Seals "Troy Seals")) – 6:04
3. "He's a Real Gone Guy" ([Nellie Lutcher](/wiki/Nellie_Lutcher "Nellie Lutcher")) – 5:05
4. "Jungle Strut" – 5:11
5. "[Blue Velvet](/wiki/Blue_Velvet_(song) "Blue Velvet (song)")" (Lee Morris, Bernie Wayne) – 4:06
6. "Ger-Ru" – 8:49

* Recorded at Van Gelder Studio in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey on November 10 (track 6) and November 11 (tracks 1–5), 1969

## Personnel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brother_Jug!&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personnel")]

* [Gene Ammons](/wiki/Gene_Ammons "Gene Ammons") – [tenor saxophone](/wiki/Tenor_saxophone "Tenor saxophone")
* [Sonny Phillips](/wiki/Sonny_Phillips "Sonny Phillips") – [organ](/wiki/Electronic_organ "Electronic organ") (tracks 1–5)
* [Junior Mance](/wiki/Junior_Mance "Junior Mance") – [piano](/wiki/Piano "Piano") (track 6)
* [Billy Butler](/wiki/Billy_Butler_(guitarist) "Billy Butler (guitarist)") – [guitar](/wiki/Guitar "Guitar") (tracks 1, 4)
* [Bob Bushnell](/wiki/Bob_Bushnell "Bob Bushnell") – [electric bass](/wiki/Electric_bass "Electric bass") (tracks 1–5)
* [Buster Williams](/wiki/Buster_Williams "Buster Williams") – [bass](/wiki/Double_bass "Double bass") (track 6)
* Frankie Jones (track 6), [Bernard Purdie](/wiki/Bernard_Purdie "Bernard Purdie") (tracks 1–5) – [drums](/wiki/Drum_kit "Drum kit")
* [Candido](/wiki/Candido_Camero "Candido Camero") – [congas](/wiki/Congas "Congas") (track 6)
